📺 THIS MARKET ISN’T BOTTOMING — IT’S STILL BREAKING

Get today's newsletter: t3live.com/rd

The market is firmly in a corrective phase, with $SPY down ~9–9.2% from highs and $QQQ down ~11–13%. Momentum and structure remain weak and broken.

This is not a clean bottoming environment — it still feels like ongoing distribution/selling, not accumulation, so beware.

*

When we are below the 8/21-day moving averages, I get defensive. That means high cash, no swing longs, and no trying to “catch falling knives”.

This is critical: you don’t fight weak structure – you step back.

*

Key Levels To Watch This Week:
These levels will define whether the market is weak or stabilizing.

$SPY
Resistance / Decision zones: 
$638–$639 → first rejection zone
$642 → gap fill area / stronger resistance

Support:
$633 → key breakdown level

Weak / Bearish Case: rally fails at $638–$639, stocks roll from green to red, market retests $633, then likely a lower continuation.

“Feeble Bounce”: the market can’t even reclaim half of the prior move, signaling extreme weakness.

Stronger Bounce (less likely): holds above resistance, fills the gap toward $642, and could trigger a 3–5 day oversold bounce.

$QQQ
Major Support:
~$561–$557 area (recent low zone). This is the most important near-term support. If it breaks, it's a continuation of the downtrend.

Immediate Resistance (Bounce Zones):
~$570–$575 (first bounce zone) → Weak rallies likely stall here 
~$580–$585 (stronger resistance) → Prior breakdown area, needs a reclaim to shift short-term momentum

Major Resistance / Trend Control 
– 21-day moving average (dynamic resistance) → As long as $QQQ is below it, the trend = bearish, rallies = sellable
– 200-day moving average (critical level) → Already lost, confirms broader trend damage

*

Why is the bottom likely not in yet?

A ~9% correction probably isn’t enough because macro uncertainty is still high. We have geopolitics (“boots on the ground” risk), layoffs due to AI, economic stress, etc.

Markets usually need more time OR more panic to bottom.

*

We might expect a bounce, but the market will dump the next day.
You could chase oversold names, but you get “sacked”.
You wait for panic, but it never comes.

So, this is choppy, punishing price action.

*

This week, keep your expectations low, be patient on rallies, stay flexible, and respect trend damage.

Markets are mixed but off the overnight lows — futures went from down ~45ish to up ~35+, so now we see if they hold, build, or fade. The oscillator hit -41, so this could be an oversold bounce to end the quarter, and 6453–6473 is the easy spot to watch if it sticks. 6525 is bigger resistance and would be hard to reclaim if the bounce tries to last a few days.

In 1980, debt-to-GDP was 26%. Today it's 122%.

There is no Volcker option when you owe $39 trillion.

This isn't stagflation. It's a depression in slow motion.
